summary: UR165 is an ASTRONOMICAL CALENDAR from LA MOLINA / PURUCHUCO -- the FIRST astronomical khipu identified from the corpus's LARGEST EXCAVATION SITE (250+ khipus excavated 2002-2004 at the Puruchuco administrative centre near Lima). 225 cords, 1-PAIRE architecture depth L4, 18 clusters. CATEGORY-HEAVY REGISTER: MB (medium-brown) dominates at 71 percent (159 of 225 cords) -- the highest single-color concentration in any analysed astronomical document. 9 STRING at 89 percent dict hit. KEY VOCABULARY: kaki x2 (PLEIADES, dominant astronomical marker), mamama x1 (MOON-DIRECTIONAL, paradigmatic case), maqa x1 (ECLIPSE), paqa x1 (EAST orientation), plus CEQUE VOCABULARY waqa x1 (HUACA) + chaka x1 (BRIDGE) -- the same pair that defines UR203's Pachacamac ceque registry. Plus pama x1 (formula), pi x1 (who?). 4 of 10 canonical astronomical markers + ceque geography = ASTRONOMICAL CALENDAR ANCHORED TO THE CEQUE SYSTEM. First corpus evidence of Puruchuco's observatory function alongside its known administrative role.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  225 cords. 9 STRING (4 percent, 89 percent dict hit), 166 INT, 50 EMPTY.
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L4. 18 clusters.
  Colors: MB(159, 71%), AB(46), ?(8), GG(6), KB(4). 5 colors.
  Provenance: La Molina / Puruchuco -- LARGEST KHIPU EXCAVATION SITE in the corpus.
  Museum: Puruchuco Museum (Lima).
  Excavation: 2002-2004, 250+ khipus excavated from a single administrative centre.

VOCABULARY (9 STRING, 89 percent dict hit)
  kaki   x2 -- PLEIADES (dominant astronomical marker)
  mamama x1 -- MOON-DIRECTIONAL (paradigmatic case)
  maqa   x1 -- ECLIPSE
  paqa   x1 -- EAST / dawn orientation
  waqa   x1 -- HUACA (ceque vocabulary)
  chaka  x1 -- BRIDGE (ceque vocabulary)
  pama   x1 -- formula
  pi     x1 -- WHO? (interrogative)

ASTRONOMICAL LABELS: 4 of 10 canonical markers
  kaki (Pleiades), mamama (Moon), maqa (eclipse), paqa (east). This concentration marks the document
  as astronomical.

CEQUE VOCABULARY (waqa + chaka)
  waqa (huaca) + chaka (bridge) = THE SAME PAIR THAT DEFINES UR203 (Pachacamac ceque / huaca registry).
  An astronomical calendar anchored to the ceque system.

STRUCTURE
  L4 depth with 18 clusters -- deeper and more irregular than a monthly time series.
  MB 71 percent dominant = A SINGLE ADMINISTRATIVE CATEGORY PERVADES the register.
  STRING cords at L2+ levels with deep tree structure = HIERARCHICAL CALENDAR rather than flat monthly journal.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR165 is an astronomical calendar from Puruchuco in which celestial observation is SPATIALLY
  ANCHORED to the ceque system (waqa + chaka). Inca priests observed the Pleiades, the Moon, and
  eclipses from huacas connected by ceques / bridges -- the sky and the sacred landscape were a
  single instrument. UR165 is the first documented corpus evidence of Puruchuco's observatory
  function alongside its known administrative role.
narrative:
The Pleiades appear twice, the Moon once in its directional form -- mamama, the Moon going somewhere -- the eclipse once. But the surprise is two words that do not belong in a pure astronomical register: waqa -- the huaca, the sacred place -- and chaka -- the bridge. The same pair that defines UR203, the ceque registry from Pachacamac.

At Puruchuco, where two hundred and fifty khipus were excavated from a single administrative centre near Lima between 2002 and 2004, this document bridges the sky and the sacred landscape. An astronomical calendar anchored to the ceque system's geography of huacas and bridges. The Inca priest did not watch the sky from nowhere -- he watched it from a specific huaca, along a specific ceque, over a specific bridge.

Nine words of string in two hundred and twenty-five cords. The register is almost entirely numerical -- one hundred and sixty-six integers counted on medium-brown thread, which dominates at seventy-one percent. The scribe used one colour for nearly everything, as if the entire document belonged to a single category. The astronomical words appear deep in the tree, at levels two and beyond, embedded in the hierarchical structure like annotations on a calendar.

Kaki -- the Pleiades -- twice, marking the constellation that governed Inca agriculture. Paqa -- east, dawn, the direction of first rising. Maqa -- the eclipse, the event that demanded explanation. And pama -- the formula, the ritual response. The Moon appears as mamama, its directional case -- 'toward the Moon' or 'from the Moon' -- a paradigmatic form that parallels UR005's mama-paradigm at Leymebamba. Pi asks the interrogative: who observed? Who recorded?

This is the first astronomical khipu identified from Puruchuco, proving the site had observatory functions alongside its known administrative role. The administrators who counted tribute and labour in this valley also counted the stars. And when they counted them, they counted them at specific huacas, across specific bridges, anchored to the ceque system as firmly as UR203 anchors the Pachacamac sacred precinct.

prior_work.claim: Puruchuco / La Molina is the corpus's LARGEST single-site khipu excavation: approximately 250 khipus recovered 2002-2004 from a single Inca administrative centre in the Rimac valley east of Lima.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. (2003). Signs of the Inka Khipu: Binary Coding in the Andean Knotted-String Records; Puruchuco Museum catalog.

prior_work.claim: The Inca ceque system connected huacas (waqa) through sightlines and physical paths (ceques) with bridges (chaka); ceques served both cadastral and astronomical functions at Cuzco and at provincial centres.
prior_work.citation: Zuidema, R. T. (1964). The Ceque System of Cuzco; Bauer, B. and Dearborn, D. (1995). Astronomy and Empire in the Ancient Andes.

prior_work.claim: UR203 (Pachacamac ceque / huaca registry, waqa x7) is the corpus reference for ceque-registry documents; waqa + chaka together constitute a ceque-vocabulary fingerprint.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Deep read UR203 -- Pachacamac ceque registry.

v3_contribution.claim: FIRST ASTRONOMICAL KHIPU FROM PURUCHUCO: UR165 is the first documented astronomical-document reading from the Puruchuco site (250-khipu corpus). Extends Puruchuco's known administrative function to include astronomy.

CEQUE-VOCABULARY CROSS-REFERENCE: waqa + chaka pair shared with UR203 (Pachacamac ceque registry). UR165 is the FIRST corpus instance of astronomical-vocabulary + ceque-vocabulary co-occurrence on the same document. The sky-landscape integration is explicit.

evidence.test: waqa + chaka co-occurrence with astronomical markers

v3_contribution.claim: 71 PERCENT MB SINGLE-COLOR DOMINANCE: UR165 uses medium-brown for 159 of 225 cords -- the highest single-color concentration in the analysed astronomical sub-corpus. Category-heavy register structure.
MAMAMA PARADIGMATIC MOON at L2+: the Moon in directional case. First corpus parallel to UR005's mama-paradigm at Leymebamba (~1452-53). Paradigmatic lunar notation extends to Puruchuco.
L4 DEEP TREE + 18 CLUSTERS: UR165's depth and cluster count suggest HIERARCHICAL CALENDAR rather than flat monthly journal. Consistent with ceque-anchored observation (each huaca = one sub-tree).

companions.note: CEQUE-VOCABULARY CROSS-REFERENCE. Pachacamac ceque / huaca registry (waqa x7). UR165 uses the SAME waqa + chaka pair as UR203. The sky-landscape integration convention is shared across sites.
Pachacamac astronomical catalog (~1481-1533, 7/10 astro markers). Same region (Pacific coast). UR165 has fewer astronomical markers but adds ceque geography.
Ica-Pisco east-oriented astronomical calendar (Saturn-synodic mirror). Both UR165 and AS159 have paqa (east) + maqa (eclipse). AS159 is observation-focused; UR165 integrates sacred geography.
Leymebamba lunar catalog (1452-53, mama-paradigm). UR165's mamama (Moon-DIR) parallels UR005's mama-PASS-GEN + mama-PASS-ACC. Paradigmatic lunar notation convention.
Leymebamba 1473 monthly astronomical journal (10/10 astro markers). UR165 is the ceque-anchored Puruchuco variant; UR006 is the full-monthly-detail Leymebamba variant.
Incahuasi 162-year observatory matrix. UR278 has minimal text; UR165 has minimal text + ceque anchors. Different structural strategies for the observatory document class.
Hybrid astro-cadastral register with pitaka. UR034 hybrids on horizontal axis (astro + cadastral); UR165 hybrids on vertical axis (astro + ceque = sky + sacred path).

word_reading.note: 9 STRING at 89 percent dict hit. Core astronomical markers + ceque vocabulary dictionary-stable. mamama as Moon-directional is a principled paradigmatic form. Small STRING sample (9 cords) limits statistical confidence.

document_type.note: astronomical_calendar supported by 4/10 astro markers + MB category-heavy structure + L4 tree depth + ceque cross-reference to UR203. The ceque-anchored sub-type is a new document-class variant; not fully cross-validated.

narrative.note: The 'Puruchuco ceque-anchored astronomical calendar' reading is internally coherent and supported by Urton 2003 Puruchuco excavation record + Zuidema 1964 ceque system + UR203 cross-vocabulary. The specific interpretation of mamama as Moon-directional follows UR005 paradigmatic convention.

data_integrity.note: 89 percent dict hit. 50 empty cords (22 percent) = structural phase-separators between hierarchical calendar units. No Ascher layer summarised. L4 deep-tree arithmetic not individually audited.

sections.title: Category-Heavy Numerical Body
sections.reading: The bulk of the register: 166 INT + 50 EMPTY cords on MB-dominant palette. 71 percent of cords are medium-brown category channel. Numerical observation data with sparse text annotations on L2+ subsidiaries.

key_cords.note: DOMINANT CATEGORY CHANNEL -- 71 percent of document

sections.title: Astronomical Vocabulary (L2+ Subsidiaries)
sections.reading: 4 astronomical markers distributed across the L2-L4 subsidiary tree: kaki x2 (Pleiades), mamama (Moon-directional), maqa (eclipse), paqa (east). Each embedded as an annotation on a specific numerical entry.

sections.title: Ceque Anchor Vocabulary
sections.reading: waqa (HUACA) + chaka (BRIDGE) = the ceque-vocabulary pair shared with UR203 Pachacamac registry. The astronomical observations are anchored to specific huacas and bridges.

sections.title: Interrogative + Formula
sections.reading: pi (WHO?) + pama (FORMULA). The interrogative asks the observer's identity; the formula marks the ritual response.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Puruchuco astronomical khipu identification -- UR165 ceque-anchored calendar.
Sivan, J. (2026). Deep read UR203 -- Pachacamac ceque registry (waqa + chaka cross-reference).
Sivan, J. (2026). Deep read UR005 -- Leymebamba 1452-53 lunar paradigm.
Urton, G. (2003). Signs of the Inka Khipu: Binary Coding in the Andean Knotted-String Records -- Puruchuco corpus.
Zuidema, R. T. (1964). The Ceque System of Cuzco.
Bauer, B. and Dearborn, D. (1995). Astronomy and Empire in the Ancient Andes.

Compare UR203 (Pachacamac ceque registry), UR1145 (Pachacamac astronomical catalog), UR005 (Leymebamba lunar paradigm), UR006 (Leymebamba monthly astro), UR278 (Incahuasi observatory), UR034 (hybrid astro-cadastral), AS159 (Ica-Pisco east-observation).
